steps.title: Attach the absolute phrase
steps.content: The sentence first names two events as important moments in US civil rights history. The phrase beginning with the former and the latter then explains what each event did. That explanatory absolute phrase should be attached to the main clause with a comma.

steps.title: Avoid run-ons and fragments
steps.content: A gives no punctuation between the main clause and the explanatory phrase, creating a run-on. C adds as before a phrase that lacks a finite verb, and D makes the explanatory phrase a sentence fragment after a period. A comma after history correctly joins the explanation. The answer is B.
